core.description: Production-bearing zones — full hardening including HSTS preload

active.description: Active zones without preload eligibility yet — HSTS without preload

parked.description: Parked/idle zones with 0 DNS records — baseline TLS only, no HSTS until they host content

current_state_evidence.always_use_https: on for ch1tty.com, chitty.cc, nevershitty.com only (3 of 12)

risk_notes: HSTS preload is one-way — once a zone is on the HSTS preload list, removal can take months. Only apply preload=true to tier=core zones with confirmed HTTPS-only operation.
Setting always_use_https on a zone with mixed HTTP/HTTPS traffic will break HTTP-only clients. Verify each tier=active zone serves traffic before enabling.
min_tls_version=1.2 will reject TLS 1.0/1.1 clients (old IoT, very old browsers). Acceptable risk for these zones.
ssl=strict requires the origin certificate to be valid. For workers-only zones (chitty.cc etc.) this is automatic. For any zone with non-CF origins, verify origin cert validity first.

rollout_order.rationale: Lowest risk — 0 DNS records means no live traffic
Worker-route-less but non-zero DNS — verify no HTTP-only consumers
Highest traffic — apply last with HSTS preload as final step
